theory.intro: When a number cannot be shared equally, some is left over. The amount left over is the **remainder**.
facts: An array or diagram shows the equal groups plus any **leftover** items.
e.g. $13 \div 4$ makes $3$ full groups with $1$ left over: $13 = 4 \times 3 + 1$.
The remainder is always **less than** the divisor.
steps: Share the items into the given number of equal groups.
Count how many are in each full group — that is the quotient.
Count the leftover items — that is the remainder.
